product: MTD115b (part of the L-Acoustics MTD line: MTD108a / MTD112b / MTD115b)

electroacoustic.frequency_range_hz: 65-14000 (+/-3 dB, LLC FRONT); 50-16000 usable bandwidth (-10 dB)
electroacoustic.max_spl_note: Per-enclosure passive system output referenced to 1 m, freefield: 122 dB cont / 128 dB peak in FRONT mode; 125 dB cont / 131 dB peak in XOVER mode (includes preset EQ and band leveling).
electroacoustic.directivity: Axi-symmetrical 85 degrees (+/-20 degrees) nominal at -6 dB
electroacoustic.components: 1 x 15" weather-resistant LF loudspeaker (3" voice coil) coaxially mounted with 1 x 1.4" exit HF compression driver (titanium diaphragm, 3" voice coil)
electroacoustic.sensitivity_db: 98.5 dB SPL (2.83 Vrms at 1 m, 65-14k Hz) in passive mode
electroacoustic.power_handling: 265 W RMS / 1060 W peak (46 Vrms long-term, pink noise 6 dB crest factor) at 8 ohms

operating_modes: Passive (single full-range amplifier channel driving the internal passive crossover)
amplification.preset_note: In passive mode the MTD115b is processed by the LLC115b-st stereo analog line-level controller, which provides three voicings: FRONT (45 Hz HPF plus low-frequency shelving, ~3 dB LF contour), MONITOR (45 Hz HPF), and X-OVER (100 Hz HPF for use with subwoofers). The original manual specifies legacy LAa amplifiers (LA15a/LA17a/LA24a/LA48a) with MLS load-matching switches; the LA4 count reflects current-generation amplification for this archive enclosure.

connection.wiring_note: Passive full-range drive on a single speakON pin pair; the enclosure can also be reconfigured for 2-way active operation.

physical.dimensions_mm: 580 (H) x 440 (front W) x 475 (D); rear width 167 mm; trap angle 41 degrees from vertical
physical.cabinet: 18 mm / 30 mm Baltic birch plywood, sealed, screwed and rabbeted angles
physical.grille: Black epoxy perforated steel with acoustically transparent foam

modes: U-bracket wall, ceiling or scaffold mount (vertical orientation)
Overhead flown via ETR2 in horizontal orientation
rigging_accessories: ETR2 adjustable U-bracket (vertical mounting; also used horizontally for flown suspension)
PION1 single-stud flytrack fitting (secondary safety attachment only, not a primary point)
Integrated flying hardware and threaded rigging inserts
deployment.rigging_note: CE declaration cites an ultimate safety factor greater than 12 for the MTD115b on ETR2 and on PION1; the recessed side fly-track accepts PION1 for safety only.

usage: Compact 15-inch coaxial point-source box for clubs, houses of worship, theaters and corporate AV where a coherent single-source wavefront and even tonal balance across the room matter more than raw output.

In passive mode each cabinet needs one full-range amplifier channel through its internal crossover; an LA4 powers up to four MTD115b enclosures, with roughly 530 W recommended per box at its 8-ohm load.
Doubles effectively as a stage floor monitor thanks to its 41-degree trap angle and the LLC controller MONITOR voicing, while the FRONT voicing suits front-of-house mains duty.
Pair with L-Acoustics subwoofers (SB115, SB118 or SB218) and switch the LLC controller to X-OVER mode to apply the 100 Hz high-pass and hand the lows to the subs for extended low-frequency reach.
The 85-degree axi-symmetric coverage stays constant horizontally and vertically, so aim it by rotating the box toward the farthest seats rather than relying on asymmetric dispersion.
Treat it as legacy inventory: it is a discontinued MTD-line product, so plan spares, controllers and rigging (ETR2 / PION1) around archive availability.
source_manual: MTD 108a / MTD112b / MTD115b Operator Manual V1.2 (EN)
